title: Authors and Citation
text: BayesianGPU Authors. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Authors B (2026). bayesiangpu: GPU-Accelerated Bayesian Inference. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/mojavedataops/bayesiangpu-core.
code: @Manual{,   title = {bayesiangpu: GPU-Accelerated Bayesian Inference},   author = {BayesianGPU Authors},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/mojavedataops/bayesiangpu-core}, }

title: GPU-Accelerated Bayesian Inference
text: GPU-accelerated Bayesian inference R.

title: GPU-Accelerated Bayesian Inference
code: library(bayesiangpu)  # Build a Beta-Binomial model model <- Model() |>   param("theta", Beta(1, 1)) |>   observe(Binomial(100, "theta"), 65)  # Run NUTS sampling result <- bg_sample(model, num_samples = 1000, num_chains = 4)  # View results summary(result)  # Plot posterior library(ggplot2) result |>   as_tibble() |>   ggplot(aes(x = theta)) +   geom_density(fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7)

title: GPU-Accelerated Bayesian Inference
text: Fluent API - Build models pipe syntax NUTS Sampler - Efficient -U-Turn Sampler Automatic Diagnostics - R-hat, ESS, divergence tracking Tidyverse Compatible - Works ggplot2, dplyr, tibble Native Performance - Rust core R bindings

title: GPU-Accelerated Bayesian Inference
text: Normal(loc, scale) - Gaussian distribution HalfNormal(scale) - Positive values Beta(alpha, beta) - [0, 1] interval Gamma(shape, rate) - Positive values Uniform(low, high) - Bounded uniform Exponential(rate) - Waiting times Cauchy(loc, scale) - Heavy tails StudentT(df, loc, scale) - Robust inference LogNormal(loc, scale) - Log-normal

title: GPU-Accelerated Bayesian Inference
text: Normal("mu", "sigma") - Continuous data Bernoulli("p") - Binary data Binomial(n, "p") - Count successes Poisson("rate") - Count data

title: GPU-Accelerated Bayesian Inference
code: library(bayesiangpu)  # Data data <- c(2.3, 2.1, 2.5, 2.4, 2.2, 2.6, 2.3, 2.4, 2.5, 2.2)  # Build model model <- Model() |>   param("mu", Normal(0, 10)) |>   param("sigma", HalfNormal(1)) |>   observe(Normal("mu", "sigma"), data)  # Sample result <- bg_sample(model, num_samples = 1000, num_chains = 4)  # Results print(result)

title: GPU-Accelerated Bayesian Inference
text: R >= 4.0 Rust >= 1.70 (building source)

title: Beta-Binomial Distribution — BetaBinomial
text: Create Beta-Binomial distribution. compound distribution success probability follows Beta distribution. Useful overdispersed binomial data.

title: Inference Result — InferenceResult
text: R6 class containing MCMC sampling results diagnostics.

title: Inference Result — InferenceResult
text: samples Named list sample vectors parameter chains Named list chain samples (list vectors) rhat Named list R-hat values ess Named list effective sample size values divergences Number divergent transitions step_size Final adapted step size num_samples Number samples per chain num_warmup Number warmup iterations num_chains Number chains param_names Names parameters

title: Inference Result — InferenceResult
text: initialize(result_list) Create new InferenceResult Rust output summarize(param_name) Get summary statistics parameter summary() Get summary parameters data frame format_summary() Format summary string warnings() Check sampling warnings is_converged() Check sampling converged as_tibble() Convert tibble print() Print result

title: Inverse Gamma Distribution — InverseGamma
text: Create Inverse Gamma distribution. Commonly used prior variance parameters Bayesian models.

title: Inverse Gamma Distribution — InverseGamma
text: alpha Shape parameter (must positive) beta Scale parameter (must positive)

title: Inverse Gamma Distribution — InverseGamma
text: distribution specification

title: LKJ Correlation Distribution — LKJCorr
text: Create LKJ prior correlation matrices, parameterized concentration parameter eta. LKJ distribution (Lewandowski, Kurowicka, Joe, 2009) standard prior correlation matrices Bayesian hierarchical models.

title: LKJ Correlation Distribution — LKJCorr
text: dim Integer. Dimension correlation matrix (>= 2). eta Numeric. Concentration parameter (> 0).   eta = 1: uniform valid correlation matrices.   eta > 1: concentrates toward identity (less correlation).   eta < 1: concentrates toward extreme correlations.

title: LKJ Correlation Distribution — LKJCorr
text: distribution specification

title: LKJ Correlation Distribution — LKJCorr
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ LKJCorr(3, 2.0)  # 3x3 correlation matrix, slightly favoring identity LKJCorr(4, 1.0)  # 4x4, uniform over correlation matrices } # }

title: Bayesian Model Builder — Model
text: R6 class building Bayesian models using fluent API.

title: Bayesian Model Builder — Model
text: initialize() Create new empty model param(name, distribution, size = 1L) Add parameter prior distribution observe(distribution, data, known = NULL) Set likelihood (observed data) model param_names() Get list parameter names num_params() Get number parameters has_likelihood() Check model likelihood to_json() Convert model JSON string (internal use) print() Print model

title: Bayesian Model Builder — Model
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ model <- Model() model$param("mu", Normal(0, 10)) model$param("sigma", HalfNormal(1)) model$observe(Normal("mu", "sigma"), c(2.3, 2.1, 2.5))  # Or using pipe syntax model <- Model() |>   param("theta", Beta(1, 1)) |>   observe(Binomial(100, "theta"), 65) } # }

text: distribution specification

title: Multinomial Distribution — Multinomial
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ Multinomial(10, c(0.2, 0.3, 0.5))   # 10 trials with fixed probs Multinomial(100, "theta")           # Parameter from model (e.g., Dirichlet) } # }

title: Multivariate Normal Distribution — MultivariateNormal
text: Create Multivariate Normal distribution. Essential hierarchical models correlated parameters.

text: mu Mean vector (numeric vector) cov Covariance matrix (optional, provide either cov scale_tril) scale_tril Lower triangular Cholesky factor covariance matrix (optional, provide either cov scale_tril)

text: distribution specification

title: Multivariate Normal Distribution — MultivariateNormal
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Using covariance matrix MultivariateNormal(c(0, 0), cov = matrix(c(1, 0.5, 0.5, 1), nrow = 2))  # Using Cholesky factor MultivariateNormal(c(0, 0), scale_tril = matrix(c(1, 0.5, 0, 0.866), nrow = 2)) } # }

title: Run NUTS Sampling — bg_sample
text: Run -U-Turn Sampler (NUTS) Bayesian model.

title: Run NUTS Sampling — bg_sample
code: bg_sample(   model,   num_samples = 1000L,   num_warmup = 1000L,   num_chains = 4L,   target_accept = 0.8,   seed = 42L )

title: Run NUTS Sampling — bg_sample
text: model BayesianModel object num_samples Number samples draw (warmup) num_warmup Number warmup iterations num_chains Number parallel chains target_accept Target acceptance probability (0-1) seed Random seed reproducibility

title: Run NUTS Sampling — bg_sample
text: InferenceResult object

title: Run NUTS Sampling — bg_sample
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ model <- Model() |>   param("theta", Beta(1, 1)) |>   observe(Binomial(100, "theta"), 65)  result <- bg_sample(model, num_samples = 1000, num_chains = 4) summary(result) } # }
